threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when a bus zooms by without stopping, not knowing it’s just how things roll in Hackney.
Last Saturday on the 36, a guy tried to convince everyone he was the next big thing in grime. Only his tracks were from 2005. The driver just shook his head.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw someone using a shopping cart as a makeshift grill in Körnerpark. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a currywurst stand next to a high-end vegan café, with patrons debating art and politics over late-night snacks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to check if the BART's closed before heading to that late-night taco spot. Tourists won't see the signs until they’re stranded.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a jazz trio set up on the 49 bus. They started playing as the doors closed at Market, and it felt like a flash mob in slow motion.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all hipster cafes and boutiques, but many of us still rely on the old-school mercats for daily groceries. Can't get that vibe in a store!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their confusion in the Mercat de Gràcia; they still ask vendors for prices in euros instead of just grabbing what catches their eye.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube'—locals just say 'the train' or 'Overground' like they're old mates.
Saw a bloke on the N35 arguing with the driver over lost property, then he just sat down and started eating cold chips from his pocket.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw my neighbor arguing with a pigeon over a stolen döner at the kiosk on Karl-Marx-Straße. You can't make this stuff up!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a club in an abandoned power plant here. It's both haunting and electrifying, like dancing in history.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to dodge the BART Civic Center exit when there's smoke, not just complain about delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni delayed by a dog on the tracks, but the best part was the guy treating it like a VIP. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the plaça, but miss the hidden murals in the alleyways. You can’t really know Gràcia without wandering off the main streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still stare at the metro map, trying to discern the differences in colours and lines.
